root_text.an5.2:2.2: Idha, bhikkhave, ariyasāvako saddho hoti, saddahati tathāgatassa bodhiṁ:
root_text.an5.2:2.3: ‘itipi so bhagavā arahaṁ sammāsambuddho vijjācaraṇasampanno sugato lokavidū anuttaro purisadammasārathi satthā devamanussānaṁ buddho bhagavā’ti.
root_text.an5.2:3.2: Idha, bhikkhave, ariyasāvako hirīmā hoti, hirīyati kāyaduccaritena vacīduccaritena manoduccaritena, hirīyati pāpakānaṁ akusalānaṁ dhammānaṁ samāpattiyā.
root_text.an5.2:4.2: Idha, bhikkhave, ariyasāvako ottappī hoti, ottappati kāyaduccaritena vacīduccaritena manoduccaritena, ottappati pāpakānaṁ akusalānaṁ dhammānaṁ samāpattiyā.
root_text.an5.2:5.2: Idha, bhikkhave, ariyasāvako āraddhavīriyo viharati akusalānaṁ dhammānaṁ pahānāya, kusalānaṁ dhammānaṁ upasampadāya, thāmavā daḷhaparakkamo anikkhittadhuro kusalesu dhammesu.
root_text.an5.2:6.2: Idha, bhikkhave, ariyasāvako paññavā hoti udayatthagāminiyā paññāya samannāgato ariyāya nibbedhikāya sammā dukkhakkhayagāminiyā.
root_text.an5.2:7.2: ‘saddhābalena samannāgatā bhavissāma sekhabalena, hirībalena … ottappabalena … vīriyabalena … paññābalena samannāgatā bhavissāma sekhabalenā’ti.

translation_text.an5.2:1.1: “Mendicants, there are these five powers of a trainee.
translation_text.an5.2:1.3: The powers of faith, conscience, prudence, energy, and wisdom.
translation_text.an5.2:2.1: And what is the power of faith?
translation_text.an5.2:2.2: It’s when a noble disciple has faith in the Realized One’s awakening:
translation_text.an5.2:2.3: ‘That Blessed One is perfected, a fully awakened Buddha, accomplished in knowledge and conduct, holy, knower of the world, supreme guide for those fit for training, teacher of gods and humans, awakened, blessed.’
translation_text.an5.2:2.4: This is called the power of faith.
translation_text.an5.2:3.1: And what is the power of conscience?
translation_text.an5.2:3.2: It’s when a noble disciple has a conscience. They’re conscientious about bad conduct by way of body, speech, and mind, and conscientious about having any bad, unskillful qualities.
translation_text.an5.2:3.3: This is called the power of conscience.
translation_text.an5.2:4.1: And what is the power of prudence?
translation_text.an5.2:4.2: It’s when a noble disciple is prudent. They’re prudent when it comes to bad conduct by way of body, speech, and mind, and prudent when it comes to acquiring any bad, unskillful qualities.
translation_text.an5.2:4.3: This is called the power of prudence.
translation_text.an5.2:5.1: And what is the power of energy?
translation_text.an5.2:5.2: It’s when a noble disciple lives with energy roused up for giving up unskillful qualities and embracing skillful qualities. They’re strong, staunchly vigorous, not slacking off when it comes to developing skillful qualities.
translation_text.an5.2:5.3: This is called the power of energy.
translation_text.an5.2:6.1: And what is the power of wisdom?
translation_text.an5.2:6.2: It’s when a noble disciple is wise. They have the wisdom of arising and passing away which is noble, penetrative, and leads to the complete ending of suffering.
translation_text.an5.2:6.3: This is called the power of wisdom.
translation_text.an5.2:6.4: These are the five powers of a trainee.
translation_text.an5.2:7.1: So you should train like this:
translation_text.an5.2:7.2: ‘We will have the trainee’s powers of faith, conscience, prudence, energy, and wisdom.’
